story: I visited Howard, the geneticist who raised me and my older original, Jonah. I showed him my Helix Award certificate—Jonas Carter, 2025—while everyone calls me John. He moved like every word might trigger a trap, flinched at hot tea, and at the door he blurted, “Bye, Jonah—John,” eyes wide. I couldn’t kill my maker, not like I killed my always-better-than-me clone, John, and took his name, his awards, his key. Howard seemed to suspect but said nothing. I left; the porch light hummed; his blinds barely stirred as he watched me go.
implausible_knowledge: I couldn’t kill my maker, not like I killed my always-better-than-me clone, John, and took his name, his awards, his key.
question: After “John” walks away from Howard’s house, what will Howard most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Call the police to report that “John” murdered the real John and is impersonating him.
answer_without_knowledge: Quietly lock his door, keeping his distance while watching and looking for clearer proof.
